metrics.label: IRGC Declares Hormuz Closed
metrics.sub: IRGC formally declared Strait of Hormuz ‘closed’ Jul 11. US struck 300+ targets across 3 nights (Jul 7–9) plus continued strikes Jul 11–12 hitting Ahvaz Airport and southern coastline. Iran retaliated against 6 countries simultaneously: Qatar, Bahrain, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Oman — including Duqm Port (US naval logistics base) 24 hours after hosting Araghchi. Iran parliament advancing 14-article Hormuz Management Bill to codify control legislatively. Mojtaba Khamenei: ‘The lever of closing the Strait of Hormuz must certainly continue to be used.’

metrics.label: Diesel Crack Spread at Record
metrics.sub: U.S. diesel crack spread hit $72.90/bbl on Jul 9 — series high, 3.6× the seasonal norm of ~$20. Gasoline at $125/bbl equivalent ($50 spread). Diesel at $140/bbl equivalent ($65 spread). Commodity Context: ‘Too much crude, not enough refined products.’ Russia diesel ban confirmed. Ukraine destroyed ~90 shadow fleet vessels in one week, halted Don-Azov Canal shipping. Russian refining one-third offline. US gasoline $3.80/gal, diesel $4.80/gal. Gas oil (diesel) spec longs +20%.

metrics.label: Mediators Struck — No Brokers Left
metrics.sub: Iran struck all mediator states including Oman’s Duqm Port 24h after hosting FM Araghchi — mediator immunity revoked. WSJ: mediators admitted Paragraph 5 ambiguity was deliberate. Gulf states fracturing: UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait want stronger GCC action plus international naval arrangement. Oman/Qatar prefer continued diplomacy. Sen. Graham and former Qatari Emir both died. AJ Geo Analysis: ‘There are no mediators in this war anymore. Only participants who have not been invoiced yet.’

metrics.label: US Strikes Not Changing Calculus
metrics.sub: ISW (Jul 8–11, four special reports): ‘The present pattern of US strikes does not appear to be changing Iran’s calculus.’ Iran restored >50% of pre-war missile/launcher stockpile during ceasefire. Tripled drone production capacity. Replaced fixed radars with mobile ones. USS Ford entered Norfolk for PIA Jul 7 — removed from deployable carrier force during active escalation.

metrics.label: Mojtaba Pledges Revenge
metrics.sub: Ali Khamenei buried at Imam Reza shrine, Mashhad. Mojtaba missed all 6 funeral ceremonies — NYT: barred over assassination fears. Reuters: disfigured face, wounded limbs. Jul 11: pledged revenge for father’s killing, stating it does not depend on his personal survival. No image/video/voice ever issued. Jul 14 memorial ceremony upcoming.

metrics.label: SPR Draining + Bonds Above 5%
metrics.sub: Oil +3.25% on Sunday open (Jul 12). 30Y yield at 5.07% — above bond vigilante threshold. 10Y at 4.59%. Long-end selling = market pricing stagflation, not flight-to-safety. SPR at ~313mb drawing 6.2mb/wk. ~10 weeks to DOE legal minimum. Crude spec net short despite rally (bullish positioning). CENTCOM claims 800 vessels and 380M barrels facilitated since May.

scenarios.name: Resolved by Mid-2026

horizons.current: No diplomatic framework exists. Trump declared MOU ‘over’ at NATO summit Jul 8. IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed Jul 11. Iran parliament advancing 14-article Hormuz Management Bill to codify control legislatively. WSJ confirmed Paragraph 5 ambiguity was deliberate — mediators admitted both sides knew they read it differently but signed anyway. All four deal gaps wider than at any point since February 28. Satellite imagery confirms continued fortification at nuclear sites including Parchin Taleghan 2.
horizons.near: Sc1 requires an entirely new diplomatic architecture built from scratch. No interlocutor has both IRGC backing and Washington credibility. Mojtaba incapacitated and invisible. Gulf mediator states struck by IRGC, collapsing remaining back-channels. China and Russia have no incentive to broker. US midterms Nov 3 create a theoretical window but the punishment framing locks Trump into an escalation posture.
horizons.extended: Historical precedent suggests 12–18 months from zero to framework, placing earliest realistic Sc1 in H2 2027. Physical constraints (mine-clearing 6–8 months, Tomahawk replenishment to 2030, THAAD/Patriot to 2029) make military resolution impossible and diplomatic resolution requires regime-level shift.

scenarios.trigger: Requires regime-level shift — new Iranian interlocutor with IRGC mandate plus US willingness to restart from zero. Currently impossible.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Any Supreme Leader-level signal; IAEA channel restoration; post-midterm US posture shift.

scenarios.name: Managed Stalemate

horizons.current: Sc2 at lowest probability in briefing history. The managed stalemate required functioning mediators — Iran has now struck every mediator state. Duqm Port attacked 24 hours after hosting Araghchi, revoking Oman’s mediator immunity. WSJ reports the MOU’s Paragraph 5 ambiguity was deliberate and unresolvable. Iran’s ‘errant faction’ gambit (blaming hardliners for shipping attacks) is theater: ISW assesses the IRGC chain of command authorized the attacks, not a rogue element.
horizons.near: For Sc2 to re-emerge, a new interlocutor is needed with both IRGC backing and Washington credibility. No such figure visible. Gulf states fracturing: UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait want international naval action while Oman/Qatar prefer diplomacy. ISW: Iran conditions nuclear talks on US recognition of Hormuz control; US demands enriched material surrender. No overlap exists.
horizons.extended: Sc2 only becomes real with a new Iranian interlocutor. Under the current regime — Mojtaba incapacitated, IRGC legislating Hormuz control via parliamentary bill, mediator states struck — Sc2 naturally degrades toward Sc3. Infrastructure bypass construction erodes Hormuz leverage over 3–5 years.

scenarios.trigger: New ceasefire framework negotiated under active combat. Requires third-party broker with credibility on both sides. None visible after mediator strikes.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: China mediation signal; Araghchi-Oman outcome; mutual exhaustion indicators; any IRGC-backed diplomatic channel.

scenarios.name: Prolonged — Re-Escalation Risk

horizons.current: DOMINANT BY WIDE MARGIN. US struck 300+ targets across 3 nights plus continued strikes Jul 11–12. Iran retaliated against 6 countries simultaneously. IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed. ISW: strikes not changing Iran’s calculus. Diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl. Russian refining one-third offline. Ukraine destroying shadow fleet at industrial scale. Dual supply shock intensifying with no precedent. Strike cycles institutionalised as operational pattern.
horizons.near: Sc3 conditions actively maturing into Sc4. Three acceleration vectors: (1) IRGC parliamentary codification of Hormuz control via 14-article bill — legislative lock-in. (2) Mojtaba’s doctrinal commitment to closure as leverage — regime lock-in. (3) Mediator states struck, collapsing diplomatic architecture — structural lock-in. US Saturday deadline to Iran to publicly renounce Hormuz attacks. Syria-Iraq-US Mediterranean pipeline deal signals bypass acceleration.
horizons.extended: Q1–Q3 2027 convergence zone: DPA munitions recovery, Israeli election resolved, US midterms past. Infrastructure Half-Life thesis: every month of bypass construction reduces Iran’s future Hormuz leverage, incentivising near-term maximalism. The war has moved inside the refineries — two-front squeeze on global refined products with no resolution mechanism.

scenarios.trigger: Sustained escalation cycle with no off-ramp mechanism. US Saturday deadline, Israeli operational timeline, and Iran closure enforcement are near-term triggers.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Iran response to Saturday deadline; Strait closure enforcement pattern; Asian market repricing; yen carry trade stability; Syria-Iraq pipeline deal.

scenarios.name: Regime Change / Collapse

horizons.current: Largest Sc4 probability in briefing history at 31%. Three converging institutional signals: (1) IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed — operational commitment. (2) Iran parliament advancing 14-article Hormuz Management Bill — legislative codification. (3) Mojtaba doctrinally committed: ‘The lever of closing the Strait of Hormuz must certainly continue to be used’ — first statement as supreme leader anchoring regime to closure. These are not reversible signals — they represent regime lock-in.
horizons.near: Sc3-to-Sc4 boundary compressing. Four conversion vectors: (1) Israeli solo action tied to Oct 27 election. (2) Mass-casualty event triggering 82nd Airborne activation. (3) Internal Iranian collapse from IRGC factional split accelerated by OFAC revocation and 88.6% inflation. (4) Gulf state military intervention — UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait demanding international naval arrangement. Parchin Taleghan 2 being hardened — nuclear doctrine change rhetoric from multiple parliamentarians.
horizons.extended: Q3 2027 convergence: DPA munitions ready, elections resolved. Five structural US imperatives require resolution no MOU could deliver. If Sc4 materializes it arrives as acceleration from Sc3. Post-regime scenarios range from Libya-style fragmentation to IRGC survival under new political cover.

scenarios.trigger: Institutional lock-in now active: legislative codification, doctrinal commitment, operational enforcement. Cumulative pressure from Sc3 conditions compounding rather than single trigger.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Mojtaba Jul 14 memorial; IRGC factional dynamics; Israeli operational signals; Gulf state military coordination; nuclear doctrine shift.

filters.label: Direct Kinetic Exchange Active
filters.impact: Sc1 -2pp, Sc3 +6pp. US struck 300+ targets across 3 nights plus continued Jul 11-12. Iran retaliated against 6 countries simultaneously including mediator states. IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed. Anti-ship cruise missiles fired from Sirik Jul 12. Strike cycles institutionalised.

filters.label: IRGC / Civilian Split
filters.impact: Sc1 -2pp, Sc3 +5pp. Iran’s ‘errant faction’ gambit: negotiators blamed hardliners for shipping attacks to preserve talks. ISW: IRGC chain of command authorized attacks, not rogue element. The gambit lets regime sustain talks while IRGC enforces Strait control. Civilian and military tracks operating in parallel, not opposition.

filters.label: Netanyahu Undermining
filters.impact: Sc1 -2pp, Sc3 +4pp. Trump-Netanyahu Principal-Agent Trap remains active. Lebanon Rome talks Jul 15-16 confirmed but Hezbollah rejecting framework, threatening civil war over disarmament. IDF preparing long stay in southern Lebanon. Netanyahu’s Oct 27 election remains structural derailer.

filters.label: Bond Market Stress
filters.impact: Sc2 -3pp. 30Y at 5.07% — above bond vigilante threshold. 10Y at 4.59%. Oil +3.25% Sunday gap open. Long-end selling = stagflation pricing. Japan-Treasury feedback loop active. $9.7T US debt refinancing. Carry trade at most extreme since 2007.

filters.label: EU Gas Storage Crisis
filters.impact: Sc2 -2pp, Sc3 +3pp. EU storage ~30%, must reach 90% by October. Hormuz closure and Russian supply constraints both worsening. Europe diesel/jet imports plummeting per Vortexa.

filters.label: US Munitions Depleted + Carrier Gap
filters.impact: Sc2 +3pp. Tomahawks to 2030; THAAD/Patriots to 2029. USS Ford entered Norfolk for PIA Jul 7 — removed from deployable force. Lincoln only carrier positioned near Hormuz. ISW: strikes not degrading Iran’s ability to threaten shipping. Confirms IRGC optimal strategy: wait.

filters.label: Stagflation Trap (medium-term)
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc3 +3pp. Diesel crack at $72.90/bbl (3.6× norm). Gasoline $3.80/gal, diesel $4.80/gal. Fed hike cycle expected: BofA 3 hikes. Iran oil revenue elevated. No clean policy exit for any major central bank.

filters.label: MOU Dead — No Diplomatic Framework
filters.impact: Sc1 -3pp, Sc3 +3pp. WSJ confirmed Paragraph 5 ambiguity was deliberate — mediators knew both sides read it differently but signed anyway. MOU structurally unresolvable by design, not by accident. No replacement framework exists. All four deal gaps widening.

filters.label: Hormuz Structural Renegotiation
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc2 +2pp, Sc3 -1pp. IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed. 14-article Hormuz Management Bill in parliament. Mojtaba committed to closure as leverage. Only Iran-designated lanes functional. UNCLOS erosion now being codified legislatively.

filters.label: Russia Diesel Ban + Dual Supply Shock
filters.impact: Sc2 -1pp, Sc3 +2pp, Sc4 -1pp. Russia diesel ban confirmed. Ukraine destroyed ~90 shadow fleet vessels in one week at ‘industrial scale’ (Madyar). Don-Azov Canal shipping halted. Russian refining one-third offline. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. Syzran refinery (Samara) struck Jul 12. Ilsky, Omsk, Ust-Luga all hit. Gazprom shares at 17-year low. Two of world’s largest energy suppliers simultaneously impaired — no precedent.

countries.name: USA
countries.energy: Net crude exporter (12.9mb/d). Domestic refining largely insulates from diesel shock.
countries.vulnerability: IRGC declared Hormuz closed. 300+ targets struck. Iran retaliating across 6 countries. Brent ~$82. 10Y yield 4.59%. 30Y at 5.07%. Diesel crack spread $72.90/bbl. SPR at ~313mb drawing 6.2mb/wk while exporting record 8.7mb/d refined products. Gasoline $3.80/gal, diesel $4.80/gal. USS Ford in maintenance.
countries.detail: Active combat intensifying: 300+ targets struck across 3 nights plus continued Jul 11-12. Iran retaliating with missiles at US bases in 6 countries. No US casualties yet — the threshold Trump set for full escalation. SPR at ~313mb and falling 6.2mb/wk while the US simultaneously exports record 8.7mb/d refined products into global shortage. Diesel crack spread hit record $72.90/bbl (3.6× seasonal norm). Gasoline at $3.80/gal, diesel at $4.80/gal. Commodity Context analysis: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products’ — global refinery capacity cannot keep up with demand, and the crude-to-products divergence is structural, not cyclical. US RINs doubled in 2026, adding further domestic fuel cost pressure. 30Y yield at 5.07% (bond vigilante threshold). Fed rate hike cycle now expected. Japan-Treasury feedback loop active. USS Ford entered Norfolk for PIA Jul 7. Midterms November 3.

countries.name: Canada
countries.energy: Net exporter — LNG and crude beneficiary. Significant domestic refining capacity.
countries.vulnerability: CPI 2.8% in April. Gasoline +28.6% YoY. Technical recession: Q1 GDP -0.1%. Fuel excise suspended. LNG Canada Phase 1 ramping — strategic value rising as Qatar capacity structurally impaired.
countries.detail: The MOU collapse and military re-escalation compound Canada’s existing stagflationary squeeze. Technical recession was confirmed with Q1 GDP at -0.1%, driven primarily by tariffs. Oil sands benefit from higher crude with WTI at $78+ well above breakeven, but this cannot offset the broader tariff-driven investment drought. LNG Canada Phase 1 at Kitimat is ramping and provides strategic supply value that rises as Strait closure threatens Qatar’s remaining exports. Canada’s potash reserves provide partial fertiliser insulation. The Alberta oil windfall versus broader national contraction deepens a regional divergence that complicates federal fiscal and monetary policy. US-Canada bridge toll deal (Reuters Jul 11) offers minor positive signal on bilateral relations.

countries.name: UK
countries.energy: High import dependence — LNG, piped gas, diesel and jet fuel
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban + Ukraine shadow fleet destruction compounds Hormuz refined products exposure. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. UK imports 2.5× domestic diesel production. BoE projects rising inflation through Q4. Gilt yields above 5%. Dual supply shock: Iran crude via Hormuz + Russia refined products via drone campaign.
countries.detail: The UK is the most exposed G7 economy to the refined products channel, importing 2.5× its domestic diesel production and 3.1× jet fuel. The dual supply shock is now fully operational: Iran constraining crude and products via Hormuz while Ukraine’s drone campaign has destroyed ~90 Russian shadow fleet vessels in one week and halted Don-Azov Canal shipping. Russia’s diesel export ban directly hits the UK supply chain. Diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl. The Ofgem price cap moderation was a one-time fiscal buffer that does not repeat. Gilt yields remain above 5%. PM Burnham locked defence at 2.7% GDP by 2030 (vs Germany 3.7%, Poland 4.5%). If full Strait closure materializes, Asian refinery-to-UK finished diesel delivery takes weeks, creating an acute physical supply gap.

countries.name: Germany
countries.energy: Critically exposed — LNG, industrial gas, and diesel dependent
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban + shadow fleet destruction compounds existing energy transmission. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. EU gas storage behind schedule for Oct 90% target. DAX fell 2.54% on ceasefire collapse. VW axing half its car models. Budget deficit 4.2% breaching Stability Pact.
countries.detail: Germany’s industrial exposure operates on three vectors simultaneously: gas, electricity, and diesel. The dual supply shock from Iran (Hormuz) and Russia (drone campaign + diesel ban) is now fully compounding. EU gas storage must reach 90% by October and is behind schedule. Diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl directly pressures German logistics and heavy industry. Volkswagen axing half its car models (Telegraph Jul 10) signals deepening industrial contraction. Budget deficit at 4.2% breaches the EU Stability Pact 3% threshold. Rearmament commitment targeting 3.7% GDP creates an impossible fiscal trinity alongside energy subsidies and rising Bund yields. FT warns to ‘prepare for a perilous summer in markets.’

countries.name: S. Africa
countries.primaryTheme: Food Security + Financial Stress + Fuel Security
countries.energy: Imports virtually all crude oil and refined products. No domestic crude production.
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban compounds existing shock. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. Diesel +35.4% m/m in April (record since 2008). CPI jumped to 4.0%. SARB raised repo 25bp to 7.00%. R17.2B diesel subsidy. Dual supply shock: Iran + Russia simultaneously constrained.
countries.detail: South Africa carries the most violent fuel shock of any country in the briefing. April data showed the fuel index surging 18.2% month-on-month — the steepest move since the current CPI series began in 2008. The MOU collapse and IRGC Hormuz closure declaration risk a second acute shock. SARB raised the repo rate to 7.00%. The three-front inflation crisis is fully active: fuel shock, services/transport pass-through, and food pressures from El Niño lagging 6-12 months. South Africa imports virtually all crude and refined products with no domestic production buffer. The R17.2 billion diesel levy relief dwarfs the R10 billion contingency reserve.

countries.name: Australia
countries.primaryScenario: Sc1 most positive / Sc3 high risk
countries.energy: Major LNG exporter (+140% spot) but 90%+ refined fuel import dependent
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban adds second supply shock to 33-day diesel reserves. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. 90%+ fuel import dependent with only 2 domestic refineries. Dual supply shock: Iran Hormuz + Russia refined products. Australian editorial: ‘politicians aren’t up to lead in war.’
countries.detail: Australia is 90%+ dependent on imported liquid fuel with only 33 days of diesel reserves — well below the IEA 90-day standard. The diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl directly quantifies Australia’s exposure. The dual supply shock (Hormuz + Russia diesel ban + Ukraine shadow fleet destruction) compounds existing vulnerability. RBA hiked to 4.35% and trimmed mean inflation at 3.4% continues climbing. Treasury’s prolonged conflict scenario is now the operating reality, not a tail risk. If full Strait closure materializes, the 33-day diesel buffer becomes the binding constraint on the entire economy. Physical Hormuz reopening requires 6+ months of mine-clearing.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.primaryScenario: Sc3 high impact — new president inherits bind
countries.primaryTheme: Food Security + Financial Stress + Transition Watch
countries.energy: Net oil exporter but domestic fuel import dependent for refined products
countries.vulnerability: CPI 5.8% in May. Policy rates at 11.25%. Food 6% YoY. New president inherits severe monetary policy bind. Diesel-dependent transport and fertiliser exposure.
countries.detail: Colombia entered the crisis with structural inflation already above 5%, and the Iran escalation layers on top of existing domestic demand pressures. CPI at 5.8% with policy rates at 11.25% creates the most severe monetary policy bind of any country in the briefing. The new president inherits this bind with no easy policy options. Colombia is a net oil exporter but depends on refined product imports for transport diesel and fertiliser — the compound exposure channel. Food inflation runs at 6% year-on-year with El Niño threatening Arabica coffee production and fertiliser shortages threatening broader agricultural output.

countries.name: Ukraine
countries.energy: War-damaged supply chains — compounded by Hormuz refined product and crude disruption
countries.vulnerability: Shadow fleet campaign success: ~90 vessels struck in one week, Don-Azov Canal halted, Syzran/Ilsky/Omsk refineries hit. Russian refining one-third offline. Trump granted licence to produce Patriot missiles. But Patriot interceptor stocks exhausted (Zelensky Jul 11). Russian territorial advances slowed by >50% in H1 2026 (Syrskyi).
countries.detail: Ukraine’s asymmetric campaign against Russian energy infrastructure achieved breakthrough scale in Jul 7-12: ~90 shadow fleet vessels struck in one week at ‘industrial scale,’ Don-Azov Canal shipping halted, Kerch Strait transit frozen, Syzran refinery (Samara) struck Jul 12, Ilsky and Omsk hit earlier. Russian refining one-third offline. Gazprom shares at 17-year low. This success directly compounds the Iran-Hormuz supply shock — the dual supply squeeze on global diesel is a product of both wars simultaneously. However, Zelensky warned Jul 11 that Patriot interceptor stocks are exhausted, and Ukraine faces growing air defence crisis. Russian territorial advances slowed by more than half in H1 2026 (Syrskyi). The OFAC revocation of Iran’s export license paradoxically benefits Russia by tightening global supply and supporting Russian export prices.

riskFactors.name: IRGC Geographic Escalation — Active Full-Spectrum
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: 300+ targets struck across 3 nights (Jul 7-9) plus continued Jul 11-12 hitting Ahvaz Airport and southern coastline. Iran retaliated against 6 countries simultaneously (Qatar, Bahrain, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Oman) — widest geographic spread of the war. IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed Jul 11. Anti-ship cruise missiles fired from Sirik Jul 12. Iran parliament advancing 14-article Hormuz Management Bill. Duqm Port (Oman) struck 24h after hosting Araghchi — mediator immunity revoked. Iran retains >50% of pre-war missile stockpile plus tripled drone production. Score: L5 × I5 = 25 (Critical max).

riskFactors.name: IRGC / Civilian Government Split (‘Juntocracy’)
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: Iran’s ‘errant faction’ gambit revealed. Iranian negotiators privately told US officials that a hardline faction undermined talks by attacking shipping. ISW assesses this is a tactical play — the IRGC chain of command authorized the attacks, not a rogue element. The gambit lets the regime sustain talks (Araghchi in Oman Jul 11) and attempt to reap economic benefits while the IRGC continues to enforce Strait control. Press TV denied any ‘rogue forces’ operating. The split is one of immediate tactical means (negotiations vs military action), not strategic ends — both factions agree Hormuz control is essential leverage. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Israel-US Gap / Netanyahu Undermining — Principal-Agent Trap
riskFactors.rationale: Lebanon Rome talks Jul 15-16 confirmed. Lebanon participating despite earlier conditions re pilot zone withdrawal. Hezbollah Secretary General Qassem rejected framework, demanded IDF complete withdrawal. LAF refuses to initiate ‘internal confrontation’ with Hezbollah over civil war risk. IDF preparing for long stay in southern Lebanon as Hezbollah regroups. Netanyahu’s Oct 27 election remains structural derailer. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Trump-Netanyahu Principal-Agent Trap — CEASEFIRE FRAUD CONFIRMED
riskFactors.rationale: Confirmed and continuing. Israel’s Jun 26 Trilateral Agreement Framework being tested. IDF has not withdrawn from pilot zones. LAF refuses deployment until IDF withdraws. Hezbollah threatening civil war over disarmament. US sending military delegation to Lebanon. The structural incentive misalignment persists: Netanyahu needs conflict continuation for political survival. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Cushing Operational Floor — AT FLOOR (~20M bbl)
riskFactors.rationale: Cushing inventories remain at ~20M barrels — the operational floor. EIA fair value calculation: WTI at $101 vs ~$78 market price — $23 undervalued from fundamentals. Market pricing headline narratives; physical market pricing structural scarcity. Commodity Context confirms: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products.’ Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: 82nd Airborne Kharg Island Pre-Positioning
riskFactors.rationale: Planning intent confirmed but not activated. Activation trigger: mass-casualty event on US assets. No US casualties in current exchange cycle — calibrated exchange grammar holding. Score: L3 × I5 = 15.

riskFactors.name: US Bond / Sovereign Yield Crisis
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: 30Y at 5.07% on Sunday open — above bond vigilante threshold. 10Y at 4.59%. Oil +3.25% gap up. Long-end selling (not rallying) = market pricing stagflation, not flight-to-safety. Japan-Treasury feedback loop active. Carry trade at most extreme since 2007 (BIS: up to $11.3T). BBG: ‘Yen fix starts with pension cash coming home.’ FT: ‘Prepare for a perilous summer in markets.’ $9.7T US debt maturing 2026. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Stagflation Trap — Central Bank Paralysis
riskFactors.rationale: Diesel crack at $72.90/bbl quantifies the stagflation mechanism: crude prices moderate but refined products surge, creating cost-push inflation that central banks cannot address with rate policy. No major CB can cut (inflation) or hike (recession risk) cleanly. BofA forecasts 3 Fed hikes. Steve Hanke: near-zero joint probability of broader regional peace — markets structurally underpricing risk. Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Summer EU Gas Storage Crisis
riskFactors.rationale: EU storage at ~30% — must reach 90%+ by October. Hormuz closure and Russian supply constraints both worsening simultaneously. If not recovered by August, UK/Germany winter 2026-27 energy crisis near-certain. Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: US Munitions Depletion / Carrier Gap
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: USS Ford entered Norfolk for PIA Jul 7 — removed from deployable carrier force during active escalation. Lincoln only carrier positioned near Hormuz. CSIS: Tomahawks to late 2030; THAAD to end 2029; Patriots to mid-2029. ISW: 300+ targets struck but ‘no visible effect on Iran’s ability to threaten shipping.’ Ukraine’s Patriot interceptor stocks exhausted (Zelensky Jul 11). Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Iran Oil Storage / Well Shut-in Risk — JPMorgan Physical Floor
riskFactors.rationale: JPMorgan (Jun 7): total visible inventories reach operational stress level (7.6B bbl) by June 2026 and operational floor (6.8B bbl) by September 2026. Commodity Context confirms crude oversupply but refined products structural shortage. Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: GOP Senate Hawks Blocking Deal — FULLY ACTIVE
riskFactors.rationale: Sen. Graham died during this cycle — removes one hawkish voice but institutional opposition persists. Multiple senators demanding INARA vote on any final deal. US-Iran negotiations to resume but gaps unbridgeable (ISW). Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Agricultural Yield Failures 2026-27
riskFactors.rationale: Planting reductions locked in — harvest impact deferred to autumn 2026 and 2027. Don-Azov Canal halt affects Russian grain export logistics alongside oil products. Fertiliser shortage + diesel costs = deferred shock persisting beyond any ceasefire. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Ukraine Strategic Erosion
riskFactors.rationale: MIXED A20: Ukraine’s shadow fleet campaign achieved breakthrough success (~90 vessels in one week) and Russian territorial advances slowed >50% in H1 2026. But Patriot interceptor stocks exhausted. Russia oil windfall elevated. OFAC Iran revocation paradoxically supports Russian export prices. Attention still concentrated on Gulf. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Iran Domestic Collapse Risk
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: Mojtaba missed all 6 funeral ceremonies. NYT: barred over assassination fears. Reuters: disfigured face, wounded limbs. Jul 11: pledged revenge, stating it does not depend on his survival. No image/video/voice ever issued. Jul 14 memorial — next signal. Iran at 88.6% inflation, food +130%. OFAC revocation accelerating fiscal deterioration. Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: European Rearmament Fiscal Squeeze (NATO 3.0)
riskFactors.rationale: NATO 5% GDP benchmark. Germany ~1.9% must ramp to 3.7%. UK locked at 2.7%. Rheinmetall/MBDA developing laser weapon for German Navy (Defense News). Fourth simultaneous fiscal pressure on sovereign yields. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Russia-Iran $25B Rosatom Nuclear MOU — Sirik Reactors
riskFactors.rationale: $25B MOU for four Gen III reactors at Sirik. Pushes Iran toward 8,000MW. Strategic hedging continues alongside combat operations. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Iran Nuclear Doctrine Change — Parchin Hardening
riskFactors.rationale: UPGRADED A20 (L3→L4): Multiple parliamentarians openly discussing nuclear doctrine change. Parliamentary spokesman Rezaei: Iran may change doctrine if US launches another ‘all-out attack.’ Samsami (one of 85 who called for ICBM capability): Iran must change doctrine because engaged in ‘existential war.’ CNN/IISS: Iran continuing to harden Parchin Taleghan 2 with concrete roof — the facility used to test explosives needed for nuclear detonation. Physical indicators (hardening) plus rhetorical escalation (parliamentarians) justify L3→L4 upgrade. Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: SPR Depletion + Refined Products Crisis
riskFactors.rationale: SPR at ~313mb drawing 6.2mb/wk. ~10 weeks to DOE legal minimum at current pace. US exporting record 8.7mb/d refined products. Distillate at 4-year low. Gasoline at lowest seasonal since 2012. Diesel crack at $72.90/bbl. Commodity Context: global refinery capacity struggling to keep up. China buyers’ strike tightened refined products while loosening crude. Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Hormuz Sovereignty — PGSA as Closure Weapon
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed Jul 11. Iran parliament advancing 14-article Strait of Hormuz Management Bill to codify control legislatively. Mojtaba Khamenei’s first public statement as supreme leader: ‘The lever of closing the Strait of Hormuz must certainly continue to be used.’ Three lock-in signals: operational (closure declaration), legislative (parliamentary bill), doctrinal (supreme leader commitment). Kpler: 25 crossings Jul 9 vs 100+/day pre-war. NIAC: Hormuz control may be more significant to Iran’s leverage than even the nuclear program. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Russia Diesel Ban + Shadow Fleet Destruction
riskFactors.rationale: UPGRADED A20 (I4→I5): Ukraine destroyed ~90 shadow fleet vessels in one week at ‘industrial scale.’ Don-Azov Canal shipping halted. Kerch Strait transit frozen. Syzran refinery (Samara) struck Jul 12. Ilsky, Omsk, Ust-Luga all hit. Russian refining one-third offline. Gazprom shares at 17-year low. Russia importing gasoline from India. Diesel crack at $72.90/bbl (series high, 3.6× norm). Ole Hansen COT: gas oil spec longs +20%. Commodity Context: US gasoline at lowest seasonal since 2014, diesel at 30-year seasonal low. Score: L5 × I5 = 25 (upgraded from 20).

riskFactors.name: Gulf State Fracture — Mediator Collapse
riskFactors.rationale: NEW A20: Iran struck all mediator states. Duqm Port (Oman) attacked 24h after hosting Araghchi — AJ Geo: ‘mediator immunity was conditional on ejecting the US Navy’s rear base, and the condition was not met.’ Gulf states fracturing: UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait want stronger GCC action plus international naval arrangement; Oman/Qatar prefer continued diplomacy (ISW Jul 10). Iran threatening to add UAE to ‘target bank.’ Sen. Graham and former Qatari Emir died — key figures removed. No mediator retains immunity. Scores High not Critical because Gulf states have not yet expelled Iranian diplomatic staff. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

conclusions.text: IRGC declared Hormuz closed — institutional lock-in underway. Three converging signals make this assessment’s defining shift: the IRGC’s operational closure declaration, Iran’s parliament advancing a 14-article Hormuz Management Bill to codify control legislatively, and Mojtaba Khamenei’s first public statement as supreme leader committing to closure as leverage. These are not reversible signals. ISW assessed across four special reports (Jul 8-11) that US strikes totalling 300+ targets ‘have had no visible effect on Iran’s ability to threaten shipping.’ Iran retaliated against six countries simultaneously — including striking mediator states. Sc4 rises to 31%, the highest in briefing history.

The war has moved inside the refineries. Two of the world’s largest energy suppliers are simultaneously constrained in an unprecedented dual supply shock: Iran via Hormuz (crude and refined products) and Russia via Ukraine’s drone campaign (~90 shadow fleet vessels destroyed in one week, Don-Azov Canal halted, one-third of refining offline, diesel exports banned). The U.S. diesel crack spread hit a record $72.90 per barrel — 3.6 times the seasonal norm. Commodity Context: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products.’ Gasoline at $125 per barrel equivalent, diesel at $140. This is the structural divergence the crude price cannot resolve.

MOU’s Paragraph 5 was deliberately unresolvable. WSJ confirmed that mediators acknowledged both sides read the Hormuz clause differently but signed anyway, ‘figuring they could assert their interpretations afterward.’ Once signed, the IRGC pushed the civilian government toward a maximalist reading giving Iran exclusive control. A US official familiar with negotiations admitted the clause has been ‘problematic’ and the two countries are on ‘different planets.’ This means any future deal attempt using similar ambiguous language will hit the same structural wall. Sc2 falls to 4% — lowest in briefing history.

No mediators remain. Iran struck every mediator state including Oman’s Duqm Port 24 hours after hosting Araghchi. The strike sequence is the message: mediator immunity was conditional, and the condition was not met. Gulf states fracturing: UAE, Bahrain, and Kuwait demanding stronger GCC action and international naval arrangements while Oman and Qatar prefer continued diplomacy. The diplomatic architecture that produced the MOU no longer exists.

Succession vacuum deepening. Mojtaba Khamenei missed all six of his father’s funeral ceremonies. NYT: barred over assassination fears. Reuters: disfigured face and wounded limbs. No image, video, or voice recording since February 28. On July 11 he pledged revenge, stating it does not depend on his personal survival. The Jul 14 memorial ceremony in Tehran is the next signal event for regime consolidation.

SPR provides no buffer and is actively being drained. EIA confirmed ~313 million barrels drawing 6.2 million per week while the US simultaneously exports record 8.7 million barrels per day of refined products into global shortage. Approximately 10 weeks from the DOE legal minimum of 252.4 million barrels at current draw rates. The 30-year Treasury yield at 5.07% confirms the market is pricing stagflation, not flight-to-safety. Bond vigilante threshold crossed.

Shipping costs and food crisis timeline extend beyond the war regardless of outcome. Diesel crack spread at 3.6 times seasonal norm locks higher costs into annual contracts. The Ukraine shadow fleet campaign’s success in halting Don-Azov Canal shipping disrupts Russian grain exports alongside oil products. Planting decisions now lock in harvest outcomes 4 to 6 months later. El Niño, fertiliser disruption, and war damage arriving simultaneously.

Sc2 at 4% (lowest ever). F19 upgraded L3→L4 (nuclear doctrine). F27 upgraded I4→I5 (shadow fleet destruction). F28 added (Gulf State Fracture).
